data_description.device_profile: Select the matching device profile for your Zendure system. The profile contains tuned control parameters.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent. Central basis for all decisions.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional sensor from the Zendure integration that reports active BMS limits (e.g. charge or discharge lock).
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of one single battery pack in kWh. This is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the battery AC power. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh (e.g. from the Zendure integration). If set, it overrides manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and further PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with current PV power in watts. Used to detect surplus.
data_description.price_now_entity: Sensor with the current electricity price (e.g. Tibber). Used for economic decisions.
data_description.price_export_entity: Sensor with price forecast data (e.g. Tibber / EPEX). Enables price-based pre-planning.
data_description.ckw_enabled: Enables direct fetching of hourly CKW dynamic tariff prices via API. No separate HA sensor needed. Prices are automatically refreshed every 30 minutes.
data_description.grid_mode: Choose how your grid consumption is measured: one combined sensor or separate sensors for import and export.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during grid import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power (positive only).
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power (positive only).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Select entity from the Zendure integration that switches between charging (INPUT) and discharging (OUTPUT).
data_description.input_limit_entity: Number entity to set charging power in watts.
data_description.output_limit_entity: Number entity to set discharging power in watts.

reconfigure_grid.title: Configure grid measurement
reconfigure_grid.description: Select the grid sensors according to the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

grid.title: Configure grid measurement
grid.description: Select the grid sensors according to the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

data_description.device_profile: Select the matching device profile for your Zendure system. Control parameters are adjusted automatically.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional status sensor from the Zendure integration to detect active BMS locks.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of one single battery pack in kWh. This is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for battery AC power. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh (e.g. from the Zendure integration). If set, it overrides manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and further PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with current PV power in watts.
data_description.price_now_entity: Current electricity price. Enables price-based discharge.
data_description.price_export_entity: Price forecast data for the next hours. Enables intelligent charge planning.
data_description.ckw_enabled: Enables direct fetching of hourly CKW dynamic tariff prices via API. No separate HA sensor needed. Prices are automatically refreshed every 30 minutes.
data_description.grid_mode: Defines how grid import and export are measured.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A combined grid sensor (positive = import, negative = export).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Zendure AC mode (INPUT/OUTPUT). Controlled automatically by the integration.

init.title: Edit control profile
init.description: Choose the area you want to edit. “General” contains shared profile parameters, “Charge” the control values for charging power, “Discharge” the control values for discharging power, and “Expert mode” additional protection and diagnostic functions.

general.title: Edit control profile – General
general.description: General control parameters of the active device profile.

data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and further PV-related logic.
data_description.TARGET_IMPORT_W: Target value for a small intentional grid import. A higher value reduces the risk of unwanted export, but slightly increases grid consumption.
data_description.EXPORT_GUARD_W: Additional safety reserve against unwanted export. Higher values make the control more conservative.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_DEFICIT_W: Minimum deficit above which discharge remains active. Helps prevent oscillation at very small loads.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_OUTPUT_W: Minimum discharge power to keep discharge stably active. Prevents unnecessary on/off switching.
data_description.SOC_DISCHARGE_RESUME_MARGIN: Additional SoC distance above the configured minimum SoC that must be reached before discharge is re-enabled after an SoC lock. Prevents oscillation between charge and discharge at low battery levels, especially in the morning with fluctuating PV power.

charge.title: Edit control profile – Charge
charge.description: Control parameters for the charging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.CHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for charge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes charging calmer, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing charging power. Higher values increase charging power faster, but may lead to less stable behavior.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when decreasing charging power. Higher values reduce charging power faster, but may cause stronger jumps.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing charging power. Higher values make charge control faster, but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing charging power. Higher values reduce charging faster, but may make it less stable.

discharge.title: Edit control profile – Discharge
discharge.description: Control parameters for the discharging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.DISCHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for discharge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes discharge calmer, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing discharge power. Higher values increase discharge power faster, but may overshoot more easily.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when decreasing discharge power. Higher values reduce discharge power faster, but may make it less stable.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing discharge power. Higher values make discharge control faster, but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing discharge power. Higher values reduce discharge faster, but may cause stronger jumps.

expert.title: Edit control profile – Expert mode
expert.description: Advanced protection and diagnostic features. First enable expert mode. Then cell voltage protection can optionally be enabled. When cell voltage protection is active, additional sensors are shown depending on the configured number of battery packs.

data.cell_voltage_warning: Cell voltage warning / emergency charge threshold

data_description.expert_mode_enabled: Enables the advanced expert area for additional protection and diagnostic functions.
data_description.cell_voltage_protection_enabled: Enables cell-voltage-based battery protection. When enabled, the sensors for the lowest cell voltage per battery pack become visible.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_1: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the first battery pack. This value is used for the global protection evaluation.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_2: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the second battery pack. This value is used for the global protection evaluation.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_3: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the third battery pack. This value is used for the global protection evaluation.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_4: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the fourth battery pack. This value is used for the global protection evaluation.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_5: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the fifth battery pack. This value is used for the global protection evaluation.
data_description.lowest_cell_voltage_pack_6: Sensor for the lowest cell voltage of the sixth battery pack. This value is used for the global protection evaluation.
data_description.cell_voltage_warning: Threshold for critically low cell voltage. Used to trigger emergency charging or protective actions early.
data_description.cell_voltage_cutoff: Hard lower cell voltage limit. If this value is reached or undershot, discharge is blocked.
data_description.cell_voltage_resume: Resume threshold above the discharge cutoff. Discharge is only re-enabled once this voltage is reached again after cell voltage protection was active.

state.expensive_discharge: Discharge due to high electricity price
state.very_expensive_force_discharge: Forced discharge due to extreme price spike
state.soc_min_resume_block: Discharge blocked (resume threshold not reached yet)

cell_voltage_discharge_blocked.name: Discharge blocked by cell voltage protection
